title: Ryser's Conjecture
statement_text: Every $r$-partite $r$-uniform hypergraph $H$ satisfies $\tau(H) \leq (r-1)\nu(H)$, where $\tau(H)$ denotes the minimum size of a vertex cover and $\nu(H)$ denotes the maximum size of a set of pairwise disjoint edges in $H$.
context_text: Ryser's Conjecture, posed in 1967, is known to hold in general only for $r=2$ (König's Theorem) and $r=3$, and for intersecting hypergraphs only for $r\leq 5$. A key open question is how tight the bound $r-1$ on the cover number of intersecting $r$-partite $r$-uniform hypergraphs can be.
paper_contribution: Constructs $r$-partite $r$-uniform intersecting hypergraphs with cover number at least $r-4$ for all sufficiently large $r$, showing that Ryser's Conjecture is close to best possible for every value of $r$.
paper_title: A note on intersecting hypergraphs with large cover number

title: Question of Abu-Khazneh, Barát, Pokrovskiy and Szabó
statement_text: Does there exist a constant $K$ such that for every $r$ there exists an intersecting $r$-partite $r$-uniform hypergraph $H$ with $\tau(H) \geq r - K$?
context_text: Abu-Khazneh, Barát, Pokrovskiy and Szabó asked this question in their study of extremal hypergraphs for Ryser's Conjecture, after establishing that intersecting $r$-Ryser hypergraphs exist for $r = p+1$ (prime power $p$) and $r = p+2$. An affirmative answer would show Ryser's conjecture is close to best possible for every $r$.
paper_contribution: Answers in the affirmative, proving that $K \leq 4$ suffices for all sufficiently large integers $r$ (Theorem 6), and $K \leq 3$ when $r$ is even.
paper_title: A note on intersecting hypergraphs with large cover number

title: Informal Problem (projective plane structure in near-extremal constructions)
statement_text: It would be interesting either to find a different type of construction of near-extremal intersecting $r$-partite hypergraphs $H$ with $\tau(H)$ close to $r$, or to show that near-extremal constructions must contain large pieces from a projective plane.
context_text: The authors observe that except for a few sporadic small examples, all known constructions of intersecting $r$-partite hypergraphs $H$ with $\tau(H)$ close to $r$ are based in some way on finite projective planes.
notes: Stated as a closing remark in prose without a labelled environment.
paper_title: A note on intersecting hypergraphs with large cover number
